text: 1.3g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.
60g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
23.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
everything else here is pretty flat.
magnesium at 46% daily value, iron at 24% daily value, potassium at 33% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first: does a lot of jobs at once. protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. cheap staple with real payoff. this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. filling for the calories. legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

on the weaker side: can take planning or prep. that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier. dry legumes especially ask more from the cook. texture is not universally loved. that can reduce how often people actually use them.
B tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
